summary:
The primary etiological agents for gingival recession include mechanical abrasion and chronic inflammatory processes driven by microbial accumulation. Among the options evaluated, "transverse fissure occlusion" (TFO) has no established link to gingival recession in existing pathological literature. Abduction-related tissue damage occurs only when associated with active procedures, which do not constitute intrinsic causes. Inflammation remains consistently cited across studies as a central driver behind progressive gum retraction. Consequently, TFO fails both mechanistic plausibility and empirical validation compared to other listed factors.  

Final decision: \boxed{D}

Among the options evaluated, "transverse fissure occlusion" (TFO) has no established link to gingival recession in existing pathological literature. Abduction-related tissue damage occurs only when associated with active procedures, which do not constitute intrinsic causes. Inflammation remains consistently cited across studies as a central driver behind progressive gum retraction. Consequently, TFO fails both mechanistic plausibility and empirical validation compared to other listed factors.  

Final decision: \boxed{D}. Answer choice: A
